zh.lifeSummary: Esther Drake於1525年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特龐迪斯福德 帕克。她於1549年與Drake Grizzell在英國英格蘭薩默塞特比特明斯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1600年在她的家鄉享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特比特明斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Drake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English drake, either ‘drake, male duck’ (compare Duck ) or ‘dragon’ (Old English draca ‘snake, dragon’ or the cognate Old Norse draki), including an emblematic dragon on a flag (compare Dragon ). Both the Old English and the Old Norse forms are from Latin draco ‘snake, monster’; its sense as a nickname is unclear but it may have had the sense ‘standard bearer’. The name was taken to Ireland in the 13th century and reinforced by later English settlers in the 17th century.
German: from Low German drake ‘dragon’, familiar as image on signboards, hence a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn with such signboard.
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized and Flemish, of Draak, a cognate of 2 above, from draak (Middle Dutch drake) ‘dragon’.

variants.name: Duck
meanings: Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Lohan (an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Leocháin ‘descendant of Leochán’) by mistranslation, as if from lacha ‘duck’.
North German (also Dück) and German (Dück): see Dueck .

variants.name: Drakes
meanings: English: variant of Drake , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: habitational name from Drax (Yorkshire). The placename probably derives from Old English dræg ‘portage, place where boats are dragged’ or dræg ‘drag net’, influenced by Old Norse drag ‘portage’, with a plural drages being shortened to drags, Drax.
In some cases perhaps an Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) cognate in some other language.

nameMeanings.name: Esther
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by a Jewish captive who became the wife of the Persian king Ahasuerus. Her Hebrew name was Hadassah ‘myrtle’, and the form Esther is said to be a Persian translation of this, although others derive it from Persian stara ‘star’. It may also be a Hebrew form of the name of the Persian goddess Ishtar. According to the book of the Bible that bears her name, Esther managed, by her perception and persuasion, to save large numbers of the Jews from the evil machinations of the royal counsellor Haman. See also Hester .

en.lifeSummary: Esther Drake was born in 1525, in Poundisford Park, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. She married Drake Grizzell in 1549, in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1600, in her hometown, at the age of 75, and was buried in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Esther Drakeは1525年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトポウンディスフォード パークで生まれた。1549年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターでDrake Grizzellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1600年に故郷で75歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Esther Drake(은)는 1525년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 파우디스퍼드 박에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1549년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트민스터에서 Drake Grizzell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1600년 그녀의 고향에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트민스터에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Esther Drake родилась в 1525 г. в местности поундисфорд парк, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла в 1600 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.
